title: Catalyst Watch: FedEx splits, tech CEOs hit Computex, Quantinuum IPO, and the jobs report
content:
[Wall Street New York stock exchange stock market]
alexsl

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - JUNE 1

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) and Globalstar (GSAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSAT]) have seen options volatility increase over the last week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Momentus (MNTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNTS]), Hyliion (HYLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HYLN]), and Redwire (RDW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDW]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Verra Mobility (VRRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VRRM]) and Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]). Short interest is elevated on Kohl's (KSS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KSS]) after its +20% earnings move. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPE]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]), Halliburton (HAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAL]), Cigna (CI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CI]), and Western Digital (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]).
 	* IPO WATCH - Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]) headlines a busy week for IPOs. The Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON])-backed quantum computing platform is looking to raise up to $1.05B and to value it at roughly $12.7B. Other IPOs expected to price and begin trading include aerospace components manufacturer Applied Aerospace & Defense (AADX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AADX]), silver miner Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining (GATO), Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]), coastal property insurer Safepoint Holdings (SFPT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SFPT]), and gas energy firm Innio Holding (INIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INIO]). The analyst quiet period ends on Hawkeye 360 (HAWK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAWK]), Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]), Rare Earths Americas (REA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REA]), and Odyssey Therapeutics (ODTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODTX]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* DATA WATCH - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on monthly deliveries from Nio (NIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NIO]), XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]), Li Auto (LI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LI]), and BYD Company (BYDDF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYDDF]). Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
 	* ALL DAY - FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) will officially split off its less-than-truckload trucking arm, FedEx Freight (FDXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDXF]), into a separate, publicly traded company while keeping the express and ground parcel businesses inside FedEx Corp. The move is structured as a tax-efficient spin-off. FedEx Freight will be a component of the S&P 500 Index right out of the gate.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day Computex Taipei event will begin. The AI-first show includes a wall-to-wall lineup of chip CEOs, heavyweight forums on physical AI, and a dense calendar of launches spanning PCs, data centers, edge, and robotics. Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) CEO Cristiano Amon, Intel (INTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTC]) CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Arm (ARM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARM]) CEO Rene Haas, and Marvell Technologies (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]) CEO Matt Murphy will all give keynotes or major presentations. Nvidia is effectively co-branding its Computex presence as "NVIDIA GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX," running across the week of the show and CEO Jensen Huang delivers a high-profile keynote. Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]) also plans a big presence.
 	* ALL DAY - Snowflake Summit 26 next week will begin in San Francisco. Snowflake's (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]) four-day flagship conference in San Francisco will be focused on "Making AI Real for Business," with heavy emphasis on enterprise data, generative/agentic AI, and applications.
 	* ALL DAY - The Atlantic hurricane season officially begins. Traders expect more volatility than normal around crude oil futures this year if any major hurricanes develop near offshore drilling assets.

TUESDAY - JUNE 2

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palo Alto Networks (PANW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PANW]), Dollar General (DG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DG]), Ulta Beauty (ULTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ULTA]), Signet Jewelers (SIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SIG]), and Yesway (YSWY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YSWY]), which will be reporting for the first time as a public company. Options trading implies double-digit swings for Oddity Tech (ODD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODD]) and Victoria's Secret (VSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VSCO]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion at the 2026 Bank of Korea International Conference.
 	* ALL DAY - The Microsoft Build event will begin in San Francisco. The flagship developer showcase will kick off with a keynote from Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) CEO Satya Nadella.
 	* ALL DAY - The two-day Deutsche Bank Basic Materials Conference will begin. The conference will include a long list of presentations from senior executives across chemicals, mining, industrials, and related basic materials companies. 
 	* 8:30 A.M. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will give remarks and participate in a conversation on monetary policy.

WEDNESDAY - JUNE 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]), Crowdstrike (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), Five Below (FIVE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FIVE]), Medtronic (MDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDT]), and Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Netskope (NTSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTSK]) and Petco Health and Wellness (WOOF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WOOF]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON]) will hold an Aerospace Investor Day event ahead of the planned spinoff of Honeywell Aerospace later in the year.
 	* 3:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
 	* POSTMARKET - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will release its May sales report.

THURSDAY - JUNE 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Ciena (CIEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CIEN]), Samsara (IOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IOT]), Rubrik (RBRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBRK]), Docusign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for X-Energy (XE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XE]) and Planet Labs (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Radian Group's (RDN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDN]) Investor Day, Solstice Advanced Materials' (SOLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOLS]) business update call, and Lucid Group's (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]) annual meeting.
 	* ALL DAY - Boralex (BRLXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BRLXF]) (BLX:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLX:CA]) shareholders will vote on the acquisition offer from Brookfield Asset Management (BAM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAM]) (BEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEP]) (BN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BN]) and Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec.

FRIDAY - JUNE 5

 	* ALL DAY - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the one-year anniversary date of the Circle (CRCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRCL]) IPO.
 	* ALL DAY - The live-action _Masters of the Universe_ movie from Amazon MGM Studios and Mattel Studios is set to hit U.S. theaters. Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) has a significant merchandising program tied to the film.
 	* ALL DAY - The Summer Game Fest conference will take place. The video game conference could generate buzz that is relevant to Sony's (SONY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SONY]) PlayStation, Nintendo (NTDOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTDOY]), Microsoft's (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) Xbox, and major third-party publishers like Capcom, Square Enix, Bandai Namco, CD Projekt, and Epic Games.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The May jobs report will be released. Economists expect 93K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.3%.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article._

MORE ON THE MARKETS

title: Billionaire Rinehart builds $97M stake in U.S. defense contractors
content:
[Celebrities Attend Melbourne Cup Day]
Scott Barbour

Australia’s richest person Gina Rinehart invested nearly $97M in U.S. defense contractors during the first quarter while also adding stakes in two mining companies.

Her privately held Hancock Prospecting disclosed new positions in RTX (RTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RTX]), valued at about $24.69M; Northrop Grumman (NOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NOC]), valued at about $24.2M; L3Harris Technologies (LHX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LHX]), valued at roughly $24.16M; and Lockheed Martin (LMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LMT]), valued at about $23.6M as of March 31.

The company also expanded its mining exposure, adding 1.35M shares to its stake in Hudbay Minerals (HBM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBM]), bringing the position to roughly 15.9M shares worth about $333.2M, while initiating a new stake in Newmont (NEM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NEM]) valued at about $20.6M.

Hancock also maintained large positions in MP Materials (MP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MP]), valued at about $717.2M, and Teck Resources (TECK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TECK]), valued at about $532.9M.

Gina Rinehart, whose fortune is estimated at $43.8B, built her wealth by developing major iron ore operations in Western Australia. Last week, her Hancock Prospecting disclosed a new investment in Rare Earths Americas, acquiring about 1.2M shares for a 6% stake in the newly listed U.S. miner. The position was valued at roughly $34M, according to a filing.

Apart from its defense and mining bets, Hancock disclosed [https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2017993/000121390026057684/xslForm13F_X02/infotable.xml]a new position in CrowdStrike (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), with the stake valued at about $16.1M at the end of the first quarter.

MORE ON LOCKHEED MARTIN, L3HARRIS TECHNOLOGIES, ETC.

 	* Lockheed Martin: The Defense Supercycle Is Here, But Not All Growth Will Flow To The Bottom Line [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4905812-lockheed-martin-defense-supercycle-is-here-not-all-growth-will-flow-to-bottom-line]
 	* CrowdStrike: Overbought Entering FQ1'27 - Structural Tailwinds Meet Premium Valuations [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4903254-crowdstrike-overbought-entering-fq127-structural-tailwinds-meet-premium-valuations]
 	* L3Harris Technologies: Strong Execution And Expanding Backlog Support Buy Rating [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4902425-l3harris-technologies-stock-strong-execution-and-expanding-backlog-support-buy-rating]
 	* Lockheed Martin Aeronautics wins $879M order for F-35 armament production [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4594669-lockheed-martin-aeronautics-wins-879m-order-for-f-35-armament-production]
 	* Northrop wins spot in Pentagon drone program as U.S. pushes mass production [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4594630-northrop-wins-spot-in-pentagon-drone-program-as-u-s-pushes-mass-production]

title: High Growth Tech Stocks in Australia for May 2026
content:
As the Australian market grapples with a challenging start to the week, influenced by global economic jitters and a dip in US indices, investors are closely watching how these conditions might impact high-growth sectors. In such an environment, identifying tech stocks with robust innovation and adaptability can be crucial for navigating potential volatility and capitalizing on opportunities within Australia's dynamic tech landscape.

Top 10 High Growth Tech Companies In Australia

Here we highlight a subset of our preferred stocks from the screener.

Clinuvel Pharmaceuticals

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Clinuvel Pharmaceuticals Limited is a biopharmaceutical company that develops and commercializes treatments for genetic, metabolic, systemic, and life-threatening disorders across Australia, Europe, the United States, Switzerland, and other international markets with a market cap of A$460.41 million.

Operations: Clinuvel Pharmaceuticals generates revenue primarily from its biopharmaceutical sector, amounting to A$96.30 million. The company operates internationally, focusing on treatments for various severe disorders.

Clinuvel Pharmaceuticals, a leader in the biotech sector, has shown promising growth with a projected annual revenue increase of 17.8% and earnings growth of 24.9%. These figures surpass the Australian market averages significantly, indicating robust potential in its niche. Recently, the company made significant strides in advancing SCENESSE, its treatment for vitiligo, highlighted by positive feedback from the European Medicines Agency on their Phase III study design. This development not only underscores Clinuvel's innovative approach but also enhances its profile amid growing demand for dermatological solutions. The firm's commitment to R&D is evident from recent interactions with regulatory bodies ensuring they stay at the forefront of biotechnological advances while catering to a substantial demographic with unmet medical needs.

REA Group

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: REA Group Limited operates an online property advertising business across Australia, Asia, and North America, offering property-related services through websites and mobile applications with a market capitalization of approximately A$21.64 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue primarily from its Australia - Property & Online Advertising segment, contributing A$1.49 billion, and its Australia - Financial Services segment, which adds A$360.80 million.

REA Group, a key entity within Australia's tech landscape, is navigating through a challenging phase with a revenue growth forecast of 6.6% per year, slightly outpacing the national average of 6.3%. Despite a recent dip in earnings by 7.1% over the past year, projections remain optimistic with an expected annual earnings increase of 13%, surpassing the broader market's forecast of 12%. This resilience is further underscored by an impressive anticipated Return on Equity (ROE) of 31.5% in three years' time, signaling strong managerial efficacy and profitability potential. The company's strategic focus on high-quality earnings and robust growth forecasts positions it well amidst evolving market dynamics, though it grapples with industry-wide challenges reflected in its recent performance metrics.

Xero

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Xero Limited offers online business solutions for small businesses and their advisors across several countries, including Australia, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, and the United States, with a market cap of A$13.32 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue of NZ$2.75 billion from providing online solutions tailored for small businesses and their advisors across multiple countries.

Xero's recent financial performance reflects a nuanced trajectory, with revenue climbing to NZD 2.75 billion, up 31% from the previous year, though net income dipped to NZD 167.42 million from NZD 227.82 million. This contrasts with an innovative push in AI through XeroForce, enhancing small business financial workflows via custom AI agents that integrate seamlessly with existing tools—an initiative likely to solidify its market position despite short-term earnings volatility. Moreover, strategic partnerships like those with Techstars and Anthropic underscore Xero’s commitment to expanding its ecosystem and leveraging AI for real-time business intelligence, positioning it as a pivotal player in transforming how small businesses manage finances globally.

title: There’s a New Rare-Earth Stock. Here’s What Makes It Stand Out.
content:
Investors have a new rare-earth stock to invest in. On Wednesday, Rare Earth Americas started trading after pricing its initial public offering at $19 a share on Tuesday. REA is a development-stage mining company run by Donald Swartz, a mining engineer by training.

Continue Reading

title: Rare Earths Americas IPO: Stock price will be closely watched today as ‘exploration stage’ firm lists on NYSE
content:
Rare Earths Americas is expected to make its New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) debut today.

Most Read from Fast Company

Applebee’s store closures: See an updated list of locations that a bankrupt franchisee wants to shutter The end of the ‘Always Available’ professional AI power users are pulling away from everyone else, Microsoft says

The company, which focuses on “heavy rare earths” projects in the United States and Brazil, will make its initial public offering at $19 per share. Here’s what you need to know about Rare Earths Americas’s IPO.

What is Rare Earths Americas?

Calling itself an “exploration stage company,” Rare Earths Americas is a critical minerals company that is positioning itself as key to creating a rare earth supply independent of China.

It plans to use the money raised in its IPO to fund land acquisition, drilling, and metallurgy, among other developments.

The company is based in Manchester, Georgia, and also has a field office in Brazil.

Rare earth elements are the backbone of everything from cell phones to wind turbines. At the same time, their extraction is also devastating poor communities around the globe.

When is the IPO for Rare Earths Americas?

The company priced its shares on Tuesday. It expects to start trading today, Wednesday, May 6, and close the offer on Thursday, May 7.

What is the stock ticker for Rare Earths Americas?

It will trade on the NYSE under the ticker REA.

What is the IPO share price of Rare Earths Americas?

The company’s share price is $19, the higher end of its marketed range. Rare Earths Americas initially estimated each share would be priced between $17 and $19 in its April 28 filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). According to Reuters, the company has a valuation of roughly $368.4 million at that price.

How many Rare Earths Americas shares are available in its IPO?

Rare Earths Americas is offering 3.3 million common stock shares to the public. The company is also offering its underwriters 30 days to purchase another 499,999 shares.

How much will Rare Earths Americas raise in its IPO?

The company expects to raise $63.3 million in its IPO.

What else is there to know about Rare Earths Americas?

In its prospectus filed with the SEC, Rare Earths Americas listed a range of risk factors for potential investors. They include notable—and almost humorous—factors given that the company’s entire aim is wanting to work on rare earth projects. The risks include:

We have no history of producing rare earth materials Mineral exploration is highly speculative and subject to an exceptionally high probability of failure All of our business activities are now in the exploration stage and there can be no assurance that we will build successful business operations or ever produce minerals from any of our properties Estimates that guide our development plans and anticipated financing needs with respect to our mineral projects may prove inaccurate or incomplete

This post originally appeared at fastcompany.com
Subscribe to get the Fast Company newsletter: http://fastcompany.com/newsletters

View Comments

title: Rightmove: A Rare Case of Untapped Pricing Power
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Pricing Power and the Munger Lens

Charlie Munger often remarked that the single most important characteristic of a great business is pricing power. Companies that can raise prices without losing customers possess one of the most valuable economic advantages imaginable. The difficulty, of course, is that pricing power is usually easiest to recognize after it has already been exercised.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 1 Warning Sign with ASX:REA. Is ASX:REA fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Untapped pricing powerif it existsis much harder to identify before it becomes obvious. Occasionally, however, a situation arises where the economics of a business suggest that pricing power may exist even though the market assumes otherwise. Rightmove may represent such a case.

Rightmove operates one of the most profitable marketplace businesses in the world. Operating margins approach 70%, the business requires very little capital, and free cash flow conversion is exceptionally high. Yet despite these extraordinary economics, the company trades at a significantly lower multiple than several global property portal peers.

Many investors view property portals primarily as advertising platforms. But Rightmove increasingly resembles something different: infrastructure for the UK housing market.

Two Types of Digital Marketplaces

Property portals around the world generally follow one of two economic models. The first resembles a transactional advertising marketplace. Platforms such as Zillow, REA Group and Hemnet largely monetize individual property listings or seller exposure. Rightmove's model is different. Estate agents pay a recurring subscription to participate in the marketplace itself. Agents are effectively paying for access to the central venue where housing supply and demand meet.

When comparing the major portal leaders globally, an interesting paradox emerges. Rightmove combines the highest margins in the group with the lowest valuation multiple. Part of the explanation lies in the structure of the UK marketplace. In many international portal markets the seller pays the platform to list a property and portals share part of that revenue with brokers.

Global Portal Comparison

Company EBITDA Margin P/FCF Revenue per Home Sold REA Group ~5055% ~40x ~$1,000+ Hemnet ~4550% ~40x ~$1,200+ Zillow ~2025% >50x ~$400$650 Rightmove ~70% ~16x ~350 (~$450)

Rightmove's model is structurally simpler. Estate agents pay a flat subscription and the company does not share listing revenue with brokers. Rightmove earns only a few hundred pounds for each home sold in the UK despite operating the marketplace where most buyers begin their search.

Story Continues

Relative to the hundreds of thousands of pounds involved in a typical housing transaction, the platform captures only a tiny fraction of the economic value it helps facilitate.

Marketplace Models Comparison

Feature Transaction / Advertising Model Marketplace Infrastructure Model Example portals Zillow, REA Group, Hemnet Rightmove, OnTheMarket (CoStar) Comparable platforms eBay, Yelp Bloomberg Terminal, stock exchanges Who pays Seller or advertiser Market participants (agents) Revenue driver Listings or advertising spend Access to the marketplace Revenue sensitivity Tied to transaction activity Less cyclical Typical margins Good High

The Behavioral Moat

The strength of Rightmove's position becomes clearer when looking at how buyers actually search for homes. Many homes are listed on multiple portals, since estate agents typically advertise properties on more than one platform. Yet buyers often begin their search on Rightmove because they are familiar with the platform and it tends to show the most results. When a buyer checks another portal and finds largely the same homesjust fewer of themthe experience reinforces the habit of starting the search on Rightmove. Over time this behavioural loop strengthens the network effect.

Housing markets function best when buyers and sellers can meet in one place with maximum visibility and liquidity. A fragmented set of competing portals would make property search less efficient for everyone involved. In that sense the industry itself benefits from a central marketplace. Alternative portals still play a useful role, providing niche listings and helping ensure that estate agents retain some bargaining power.

The result is a stable equilibrium: the market gravitates toward one primary marketplace while secondary portals exist at the margins.

UK Portal Usage Snapshot

Portal Homes for Sale Monthly Visits Rightmove ~800k ~120M Zoopla ~700k ~55M OnTheMarket ~450k ~25M

How Rightmove Actually Grows

Because the number of estate?agent branches in the UK is relatively stable, growth does not primarily come from adding new customers. Instead, the key driver is ARPAaverage revenue per advertiser. Rightmove increases ARPA through gradual price increases, premium listing products, and marketing or data tools for agents.

Risks

The most credible competitive challenge in recent years has come from CoStar Group, which acquired OnTheMarket. Unlike earlier competitors, CoStar has the financial resources to fund large marketing campaigns and attempt to build consumer traffic. However, the key question is not whether a competing portal can be built, but whether estate agents feel confident enough to leave Rightmove without losing buyers.

Investor pressureincluding from hedge fund manager Daniel Loeb (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio)may also constrain how aggressively CoStar pursues expansion in residential portals compared with its highly profitable commercial real estate data business.

A structural risk comes from supply-side consolidation. The UK market is highly fragmented with many thousands of agents with local branches selling a few dozen homes per month. Any signifcant consolidation directly afects Rightmove's revenue and pricing power.

Final Thoughts

Rightmove already operates one of the most profitable marketplace models in the global portal industry, yet it captures a relatively small share of the housing transaction compared with some international peers.

Markets that connect highly fragmented participants often gravitate toward a single dominant venue. Once that venue becomes the default place where transactions begin, the owner of the marketplace tends to capture an increasing share of the economic value over time. As long as this equilibrium holds, Rightmove's position begins to resemble infrastructure rather than a conventional marketplace.Rightmove: A Rare Case of Untapped Pricing Power

title: REA Group (ASX:REA) Will Pay A Dividend Of A$1.24
content:
REA Group Limited (ASX:REA) will pay a dividend of A$1.24 on the 18th of March. This will take the annual payment to 1.6% of the stock price, which is above what most companies in the industry pay.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

REA Group's Payment Could Potentially Have Solid Earnings Coverage

If the payments aren't sustainable, a high yield for a few years won't matter that much. Prior to this announcement, REA Group's dividend was comfortably covered by both cash flow and earnings. This means that a large portion of its earnings are being retained to grow the business.

Looking forward, earnings per share is forecast to rise by 53.3% over the next year. If the dividend continues along recent trends, we estimate the payout ratio will be 44%, which is in the range that makes us comfortable with the sustainability of the dividend.ASX:REA Historic Dividend February 11th 2026

See our latest analysis for REA Group

REA Group Has A Solid Track Record

The company has been paying a dividend for a long time, and it has been quite stable which gives us confidence in the future dividend potential. Since 2016, the dividend has gone from A$0.70 total annually to A$2.76. This means that it has been growing its distributions at 15% per annum over that time. Rapidly growing dividends for a long time is a very valuable feature for an income stock.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

The company's investors will be pleased to have been receiving dividend income for some time. It's encouraging to see that REA Group has been growing its earnings per share at 33% a year over the past five years. The company doesn't have any problems growing, despite returning a lot of capital to shareholders, which is a very nice combination for a dividend stock to have.

We Really Like REA Group's Dividend

Overall, a dividend increase is always good, and we think that REA Group is a strong income stock thanks to its track record and growing earnings. The company is easily earning enough to cover its dividend payments and it is great to see that these earnings are being translated into cash flow. All in all, this checks a lot of the boxes we look for when choosing an income stock.

Companies possessing a stable dividend policy will likely enjoy greater investor interest than those suffering from a more inconsistent approach. Still, investors need to consider a host of other factors, apart from dividend payments, when analysing a company. Earnings growth generally bodes well for the future value of company dividend payments. See if the 15 REA Group analysts we track are forecasting continued growth with our freereport on analyst estimates for the company. Looking for more high-yielding dividend ideas? Try our collection of strong dividend payers.
